commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an opt-in Volcengine `params.cache` stream wrapper for Ark Responses `previous_response_id` chaining, unit tests, and English docs examples.

PR surface: Source +393, Tests +210, Docs +67. Total +670 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. for the central feature request; the PR is adding opt-in Volcengine session caching rather than fixing a reproduced bug. The blocking review findings are source-reproducible from the added cache wrapper and current-main transport contracts.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers include a new provider cache/privacy contract and a dirty branch, not only mechanical code edits.

target.title: feat(extension): support session cache for volcengine
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: - Problem: Volcengine provider lacked session-based response caching support for multi-turn conversations. - Why it matters: Without session caching, every turn sends full context to the API, increasing latency and cost for Volcengine Doubao models. - What changed: Added extensions/volcengine/cache.ts with session cache logic — manages previous_response_id, caching, thinking, store, and expire_at fields automatically. Configured via params.cache: { enable: true, ttlSec?, thinking? }. Wired into the provider via wrapStreamFn in index.ts. (cache.test.ts, 27 tests). Updated docs with config examples. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Provider registration, catalog, auth flow unchanged. No changes outside extensions/volcengine/ and docs. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## User-visible / Behavior Changes List user-visible changes (including defaults/config). Once enable cache and openai-responses, we can see the cache read token from session file for each LLM request config example： ``` "models": { "providers": { "volcengine": { "api": "openai-responses" } } }, "agents": { "defaults": { "models": { "volcengine/xxxx": { "alias": "volcengine", "params": { "cache": { "enable": false, "ttlSec": 3600, "thinking": true } } } } } } ``` session file example: ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: ## Repro + Verification ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: enable cache, and check the cached read token - Edge cases checked: Once the cache token expired by set the ttl with 120s, OpenClaw will retry the LLM request and cache token agent once receive new message - What you did **not** verify: ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes`) - Config/env changes? (`Yes`) - Migration needed? (`No`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: - Mitigation:
target.author: stayrascal
